title: The Emperor's Pawns

"'''The Emperor's Pawns'''" was an article appearing in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]], written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]], under the name [[Juan Schwartz]], and illustrated by [[Joe Corroney]].

The article provided background information on the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]] who served [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], such as [[Sarcev Quest]] and [[Jeng Droga]]. It was Peña's first published ''Star Wars'' article.

==Adventure seeds {{SectionFlag}}==
A number of adventure seeds were included with the article that contained elements that have worked their way into [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] since the original release by Abel G. Peña:

*[[The Emperor's Pawns|''Omnipotent Mastermind'']]
*[[The Emperor's Pawns|''The Lost Art'']]
*''[[Sinister Alliance]]''
*[[Unfinished Business (The Emperor's Pawns)|''Unfinished Business'']]
*''[[Life After Death]]''
*[[In Too Deep (The Emperor's Pawns)|''In Too Deep'']]
*''[[Droga's Destiny]]''

==Development==
[[Cronal|Blackhole's]] entry was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] for the ''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'',<ref name="blog2">{{Blog|abelgpena|69|The Emperor's Pawns: A Retrospective}}</ref> although ''The Emperor's Pawns'' was publishd before<ref name="SW.com" /> the ''Rebellion Era Sourcebook''.<ref name="Date">{{Wizards|url=starwars/product.asp?WTC11837|text=Rebellion Era Sourcebook}}</ref>

The name of the endnotes article on [[StarWars.com]], "Blackhole and Revelations," is a play on the name of the [[2006]] album [[Wikipedia:Black Holes and Revelations|''Black Holes And Revelations'']] by the English rock band [[Wikipedia:Muse (band)|Muse]].
